steps.title: Name the needed evidence
steps.content: The underlined explanation claims that Mexican art objects reached Missouri through long-distance trade, allowing a local artist to adopt jaguar imagery for the Fairfield Gorget. To support that explanation, evidence should connect Mexican art, the Mississippian period, and the area near where the gorget was found.

steps.title: Test the findings
steps.content: Choice A redates the gorget to an earlier period, weakening the proposed Mississippian context. Choice B concerns jaguar range after the relevant period. Choice D discusses modern artists, not ancient trade. A Mexican art object found nearby from the same period would directly support the trade route. The answer is C.
